page: Amazon Web Services t4g.micro is a burstable performance instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features an arm64 architecture utilizing the AWS Neoverse-N1 CPU model running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is provisioned with 2 virtual CPUs corresponding to 2 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, indicating no hyperthreading. It includes 1.0 GB of system memory, yielding 0.5 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications include 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance runs on the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate modest performance characteristics. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance registers at 1518.88, placing it in the average tier, while multi-core performance drops to a weak rating of 3008.28. Memory bandwidth tests show 116.9 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 34.19 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Passmark CPU and memory scores are low, with a CPU score of 1331.42 and a memory score of 798.83. Database operations under Passmark score 1242.78, and Redis SET operations achieve 2.16 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is measured at 2.11 GB/sec.

The t4g.micro offers a highly resource-dense, entry-level hardware profile designed for budget-conscious deployments. The primary tradeoff is its burstable CPU allocation and low memory capacity, which limits sustained heavy computations. Given the 1.0 GB memory limit and weak multi-core performance, this instance is best suited for low-traffic web serving, lightweight background tasks, microservices, and basic development or testing environments that do not require continuous high CPU utilization.
description: Amazon Web Services t4g.micro is a burstable, entry-level instance powered by a 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor with an arm64 architecture. It features 2 vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 1.0 GB of system memory, and runs on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Benchmark results show average single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 1518.88, but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities, including a Passmark CPU score of 1331.42 and a memory score of 798.83. This resource profile is qualitatively highly cost-efficient for low-demand workloads. It is best suited for lightweight tasks such as basic web serving, microservices, and development environments that tolerate burstable CPU limits and low memory capacity.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t4g.micro is a burstable arm64 instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 1.0 GB of RAM, and AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processors, designed for lightweight workloads and budget-sensitive hosting.

tagline: A burstable arm64 micro instance offering entry-level compute and memory resources for lightweight, budget-conscious development and testing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 arm64 processors at 2.5 GHz
Features 2 vCPUs, 1.0 GB RAM, and 32 MB L3 cache on the Nitro hypervisor
Burstable CPU allocation with average single-core and weak multi-core performance
No local storage and 0 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Suited for low-traffic web serving, microservices, and testing environments
